factors.name: Fed Policy Pivot (Dovish FedSpeak)
factors.summary: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh delivered softer remarks, dampening speculation on rate hikes this year. Bloomberg reported 'Gold Extended Gain After Warsh Remarks Ease Rate-Hike Prospects.' This is a material dovish surprise reducing real yield expectations.

factors.name: Real Yields (Inverse Driver)
factors.summary: Lower real yields (proxied by softer hike expectations) are the single most correlated bullish driver for gold. Warsh's remarks imply a flatter rate path, compressing the 10Y real yield.

factors.name: USD Weakness (DXY Indirect)
factors.summary: GBP/USD rallied to 1.3290, EUR/USD nudged above 1.1380, and broader USD softness reported as traders moved off hawkish USD positioning. Weaker USD is bullish for USD-denominated gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Ukraine/Iran)
factors.summary: Russia launched major missile strikes on Kyiv (at least 2 killed); Iran-US talks showed 'positive progress' with de-escalation bias. Mixed headline: Ukraine risk supports safe-haven bid, but Iran de-escalation eases energy premium (bearish oil, but neutral-to-bullish gold as growth-risk recedes).

factors.name: Oil Prices (Inflation Proxy Down)
factors.summary: WTI slipped below $68, Brent fell to lowest since pre-Middle East war start. Oil decline reduces near-term inflation risk, supporting Fed's case for patience on rates—tailwind for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (Equities Weakness)
factors.summary: South Korea KOSPI dumped 6%, Japan Nikkei down 2.5%+, US Nasdaq declined sharply on Wed. Chip/AI selloff ($137bn foreigner outflows from Asia) signals risk-off; gold as safe-haven bid benefits.

factors.name: US NFP Coming (High-Impact Event Today/Thu)
factors.summary: June NFP expected to slow sharply to 110k vs May's 172k (per CBA preview). Major print looms in ~24h; markets treading water. Data could swing bias hard—weak NFP bullish gold, hot print bearish.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent Soft Signals)
factors.summary: No recent CPI/PCE print in news window, but oil decline and broader commodity weakness suggest easing inflation narrative. No hot surprise visible.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new CB buying reported in this news cycle. Structural bid present but not active signal.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU holdings data in this window. Institutional positioning unclear.

key_levels.support: $4,050 (7-month lows, recent bounce point per FXStreet)
key_levels.resistance: $4,100 (psychological level; prior swing high before recent selloff)

screening.narrative: Gold rebounded +2% in 24h from near 7-month lows on dovish Fed pivot signaled by Chairman Warsh, who dampened rate-hike speculation and eased real yield expectations. Risk-off equity action (KOSPI -6%, chip selloff, $137bn Asia outflows) reinforces safe-haven bid, while lower oil prices (WTI <$68) reduce inflation pressure and support the case for patient Fed policy. However, market is cautiously holding ahead of critical June NFP data (expected ~110k, sharply below May's 172k) due today/tomorrow—a surprise hot print could invert the bias quickly.
catalysts_ahead: US June NFP (non-farm payrolls) — due today/Thursday, market-moving, expected 110k vs 172k prior
PBOC commentary & liquidity operations (ongoing, yuan fixing at 6.8088 shows slight weakness pressure)
Bank of Korea decision (July 16) — inflation at 2.5y high, rate hike odds rising; impacts yen carry unwinds
Additional Fed speakers (Warsh tone shift is fresh; watch for contradictory Powell/Waller remarks)
Broader chip/AI sentiment reset — if Meta/Apple demand fears ease, equity stabilization could trigger profit-taking in gold
screening.risk_warning: NFP surprise (>150k) would flip bias to BEARISH gold and reassert hawkish Fed pricing, crushing real yield tailwind. Equally, risk-on reversal (equity stabilization, broader EM sentiment recovery) could deflate safe-haven demand. Move is already +2% in 24h (Phase 2), so extended rally risks pullback entry—wait for retest of $4,050 support or NFP clarity before fresh longs. USD strength reversal (if Fed messaging hardens again) would undermine move.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (+2% in 24h); avoid chasing here. Wait for retest/support hold at $4,050–$4,060 after NFP, or if NFP disappoints sharply (<80k), breakout over $4,100 with conviction. If NFP beats, expect gold to face headwinds—consider SHORT on spike above $4,090.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Real Yields
factors.summary: Bloomberg Markets (02:28) reports gold extended gains after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh remarks dampened rate-hike speculation, easing real yield pressure. EUR/USD (00:29) confirms 'softer Fed stance' from Warsh comments, which typically compresses real yields and supports gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: Multiple FX sources (EUR/USD to 1.1380, GBP/USD to 1.3290, NZD/USD at one-week high) show USD softness across majors following Warsh's less hawkish tone. Weaker DXY is structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: US Top News (02:01) and Investinglive (22:25) report oil fell 1%+ as Middle East tensions ease after US-Iran Doha talks concluded with 'positive progress' per Qatar (FXStreet 23:29). Kyiv missile strikes (BBC 03:08) provide some safe-haven bid but Middle East de-escalation removes key gold support—oil hit lowest since before war started.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP Data
factors.summary: Investinglive (02:35) flags US payrolls preview with June NFP expected at 110k (down from May's 172k surge). Multiple FX sources cite traders waiting for jobs data. Strong NFP surprise would revive Fed hike expectations and pressure gold; weak print would support current dovish repricing.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Selloff
factors.summary: Massive tech/chip selloff: Korea KOSPI -6%, Japan Nikkei -2.5% (Investinglive 00:49), Samsung/SK Hynix -7%+ (US Top News 03:04). Meta cloud plans and Apple-China chip talks rattled AI trade. Risk-off equity flows typically support gold as safe-haven, though USD strength can compete.

factors.name: China Gold Demand / CNY Weakness
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY fix at 6.8088 vs 6.7929 estimate (Investinglive 01:17), showing continued yuan weakness. Weaker CNY can reduce Chinese physical gold demand but also signals PBoC may lean dovish. No specific central bank buying headlines in this cycle.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI below $68, down third day (FXStreet 01:01). Brent at lowest since before Middle East war (Bloomberg 22:05). Collapsing oil reduces inflation expectations and real-economy gold hedging demand, though it also supports dovish Fed pivot narrative.

factors.name: Nominal Yields / Curve
factors.summary: Investinglive (21:41) notes bund curve flattened alongside crude drop. No specific US 10Y movement cited, but Warsh dovish tilt likely capped long-end yields. Awaiting NFP for directional conviction.

factors.name: JPY Intervention Risk
factors.summary: USD/JPY around 162.50-162.55 with repeated warnings of Japanese intervention (FXStreet 01:16, 22:30). Yen weakness typically bullish for gold via carry-trade liquidity, but intervention threat creates two-way volatility.

factors.name: Move Already Underway
factors.summary: Gold is +2.00% in 24h, rebounding from seven-month low. Warsh comments triggered the rally; move is in Phase 2. Bloomberg (00:28) confirms gold 'extended gain' after remarks—much of the dovish repricing is now in price.

confirmation.narrative: Gold rebounded +2% from seven-month lows after Fed Chair Warsh's less hawkish remarks dampened rate-hike expectations, compressing real yields and weakening the dollar. However, this dovish repricing is largely priced in (Phase 2 move). Simultaneously, easing Middle East tensions sent oil to pre-war lows, stripping out geopolitical premium and inflation expectations—a meaningful cross-current. The massive Asia equity selloff (KOSPI -6%) provides some safe-haven bid, but upcoming NFP data (hours away) is the decisive catalyst: a hot print would reignite Fed hike fears and reverse recent gains; a weak print would validate the dovish pivot. Current price action reflects a tug-of-war between dovish Fed repricing (bullish) and collapsing geopolitical/inflation premium (bearish).
catalysts_ahead: US NFP jobs report for June (due imminently, expected 110k vs 172k prior)
US-Iran indirect talks outcome clarity on Strait of Hormuz tolls
Potential Japanese FX intervention at USD/JPY 162-165 zone
Further Fed speakers clarifying Warsh's stance
China trade and macro data flow (Australia deficit signals weak commodity demand)
confirmation.risk_warning: NFP surprise above 150k+ would likely trigger rate-hike repricing, strengthen USD, and reverse the current rally. Conversely, if Middle East tensions re-escalate (Hormuz closure, Iran toll enforcement), oil spike would restore geopolitical premium. Phase 2 positioning risk: much of the Warsh-dovish move is done; chasing here without confirmation risks buying the top of a dead-cat bounce before NFP.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2—wait for NFP reaction. If data is weak and gold dips to 4020-4030 on profit-taking, that offers better risk/reward for longs. If NFP is hot, look for breakdown below 4000 to confirm bearish reversal.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=BUY vs Sonnet=NEUTRAL — silent log
